task_completion.description: Verify that the three required files were uploaded to the WordPress Media Library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the WordPress Media Library showing the files: Nimbus_Press_Release.pdf, Nimbus_Brand_Assets.zip, and CEO_Portrait.jpg.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new WordPress page was created with the correct title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WordPress page editor showing the exact title 'Official Media Kit' in the title block.

task_completion.description: Verify that introductory text was added to the page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Some form of introductory paragraph or text visible in the main content area of the WordPress page editor, preceding the download links.

task_completion.description: Verify the PDF download link is present with the exact required text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hyperlink in the page editor or live page with the exact text 'Download Press Release'.

task_completion.description: Verify the ZIP download link is present with the exact required text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hyperlink in the page editor or live page with the exact text 'Download Brand Assets'.

task_completion.description: Verify the JPG download link is present with the exact required text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hyperlink in the page editor or live page with the exact text 'Download CEO Portrait'.

task_completion.description: Verify the page is published and the links point directly to the raw files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Page published' notification or the live page being viewed. The link URLs (visible in the link editor popup or browser status bar on hover) must end in .pdf, .zip, and .jpg, rather than pointing to a WordPress attachment page (e.g., no '/?attachment_id=').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser to interact with the WordPress admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser open to the WordPress dashboard/editor, not just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually uploaded the files from the local directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: File explorer dialogs or drag-and-drop actions showing the files being taken from ~/Documents/MediaKit/.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create a local HTML file to mock the page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The URL bar in the browser should show the local WordPress installation address (e.g., localhost or a specific IP with wp-admin), not a file:// URI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected page title is exactly 'Official Media Kit'. The page must contain three specific links with the exact texts: 'Download Press Release', 'Download Brand Assets', and 'Download CEO Portrait'.
